content: How should the inbox handle scaling when processed by another system? The current single-file-per-session JSONL format requires scanning every session file and checking processed flags per line. Three alternatives were proposed: (1) one file per message with atomic pickup/delete, (2) separate pending/done directories with file-move on completion, (3) append-only log with offset tracking. No direction chosen yet.

provenance.raw_agent: However, there are still some potential performance concerns with the current structure: 1. One file per session — a processor scanning for unprocessed messages has to open every session file... Some alternative approaches that would scale better: One file per message, Separate pending/ and done/ directories, Append-only log + offset tracking
provenance.raw_user: Are all the messages in a single JSON object in a single file? If they are being processed by another system, isn't that going to cause a performance degradation?
